summary: Tested OTP and Fallback OTP functionality for customer registration. Fixed 1 bug in CustomerRegister.jsx (states.map is not a function). All backend endpoints pass (15/15 tests). All frontend flows work correctly.

fixed_during_testing.issue: TypeError: states.map is not a function - API returns {states: [...]} but code expected direct array
fixed_during_testing.fix_applied: Updated fetchStates and fetchDistricts to handle both object and array response formats

seed_data_creation: None - used existing fallback OTPs from seed endpoint
context_for_next_testing_agent: OTP and Fallback OTP functionality fully tested. Customer registration 5-step flow works: Phone->OTP->Role->Details->Success. Settings page Fallback OTP management (Add/Toggle/Delete) works. The WhatsApp OTP uses real BotMasterSender API but may fail for unregistered numbers - fallback OTPs solve this. Existing fallback OTPs: 0101, 3355, 8796, 5896, 5478, 9635, 1596, 1478, 9630, 8520, 7410, 1234, etc.

elements_verified: Button disabled when empty, enabled with 10 digits

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: API endpoint /api/public/states returns {states: [...]} object, but frontend code tried to call .map() directly on response.data
reproduction_steps: 2. Enter phone number and send OTP
5. On Step 4 Details form, the states.map error crashes the page
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Updated fetchStates and fetchDistricts functions to check if response.data is array or object and extract appropriately
